title: Catching the bus
desc: Ride the urban bus and pay the fare
setting: A crowded bus pulls up. You squeeze on and the cobrador (fare collector) works his way toward you.

steps.speaker: Cobrador
steps.en: Fare, fare! Where to, young man?

options.feedback: Destination plus the fare question — efficient, exactly right for a packed bus.
Good — he'll tell you the fare. Asking the price yourself is even smoother.
On a moving bus he needs a quick answer. Have your stop ready.
Quick and clear — hand over the coins and you're set.

options.en: Do you have change for a hundred?
options.feedback: Fair to ask, but small bills are gold on buses. Try to have coins ready.
Five córdobas is the fixed fare — there's no haggling on the bus!

steps.speaker: Señora a la par
steps.es: Agárrese bien, que este chofer maneja recio.
steps.en: Hold on tight, this driver drives hard.

options.feedback: Friendly — strangers look out for each other on the bus. Accept the kindness.
Nice, natural reply — shares the moment with a little humor.
Yikes — she's being kind! That would be rude here, where buses are social.

steps.speaker: Cobrador
steps.es: ¡Centro! El que se baja, que se baje ya.
steps.en: Downtown! Whoever's getting off, get off now.

options.feedback: 'Permiso' to squeeze past the crowd is essential bus etiquette. Perfect.
Clear and loud enough to be heard — just what's needed.
The bus won't wait! You have to move quickly at your stop.
